text.title: Django Celery Task Rules
text.summary: Mandatory attributes, idempotency pattern, time limits, and calling conventions for Celery tasks in Django.

rule.statement: Every task must declare: explicit name, bind=True, max_retries, autoretry_for with retry_backoff=True and retry_jitter=True, soft_time_limit, and time_limit. Tasks without these attributes are rejected in code review.
rule.rationale: Implicit names break on module rename. Missing bind=True makes self.retry() crash. Missing time limits let slow tasks block worker slots indefinitely. Missing retry config turns transient failures into permanent ones.

example.code:
from celery import shared_task import requests
@shared_task( name="emails.send_welcome", bind=True, max_retries=5, autoretry_for=(requests.RequestException,), retry_backoff=True, retry_backoff_max=600, retry_jitter=True, soft_time_limit=300, time_limit=360, ) def send_welcome(self, user_id: int) -> bool: """Send welcome email. Idempotent — checks sent flag before action.""" from apps.users.models import User user = User.objects.only("id", "email", "welcome_email_sent").get(pk=user_id) if user.welcome_email_sent: return False # already done send_email(user.email, "Welcome!") User.objects.filter(pk=user_id, welcome_email_sent=False).update( welcome_email_sent=True ) return True
example.note: The idempotency guard uses UPDATE WHERE not_done=True — a DB-level atomic check-and-set, not a read-then-write race.

rule.statement: Tasks accept only primitive arguments (int, str, float, bool, dict of primitives). Never pass model instances — they serialize to stale data and fail to deserialize on worker restart.
rule.rationale: ORM objects serialized into the queue carry the state at call time. By the time the worker runs, the DB row may have changed. IDs force a fresh query inside the task.

rule.statement: Route tasks by workload class: emails, webhooks, reports, long_running. Each queue maps to one SLA. Never mix fast and slow tasks on the default queue.
rule.rationale: Long-running tasks block worker concurrency slots. A slow report task sharing a queue with email tasks can delay all email delivery.

section.title: Calling Conventions

rule.statement: Use .delay() for simple async dispatch. Use .apply_async() when you need countdown, eta, or explicit queue. Never call tasks synchronously in production request paths.
rule.rationale: Synchronous task execution in a request thread defeats the purpose of async processing and blocks the web worker.

section.reference: Canonical idempotent task with full retry and time-limit config
Systemd unit for a per-queue worker

rule.statement: Use RedBeat or django-celery-beat with leader election for periodic tasks. Never run multiple naive celery beat processes — they produce duplicate executions.
rule.rationale: On HA deployments, two beat processes both fire the same schedule, causing duplicate emails, double-charges, or corrupt aggregations.
